name: Chickpea-Couscous Croquettes
recipeInstructions:
If using sunflower seeds, preheat oven to 350°F. Spread seeds on a baking tray. Remove any shells or discolored seeds. Bake 5-7 minutes, until the seeds smell nutty and darken slightly. Grind in a food processor until coarsely chopped. Add chickpeas and process until well mixed. Keep the mixture in the food processor.
In a heavy pot, combine couscous, tomato juice, and red wine. Stir and bring to a boil. Lower heat, cover, and simmer 2-3 minutes, until couscous has absorbed all the liquid. Let stand for 5 more minutes. Add cooked couscous to chickpea mixture along with remaining ingredients except oil. Mix well, stopping the processor and scraping down the sides once or twice, until smooth.
Lightly oil your hands. Shape about 2-3 tablespoons of chickpea mixture between your hands into balls. Repeat with remaining mixture until you have 24 balls. Flatten the balls to form patties about 2 inches wide and 1/2 inch thick.
Brush croquettes with olive oil and place on a lightly oiled baking tray. Bake for 15 minutes, turn over, brush with oil again, and bake another 10-12 minutes. You can also bake croquettes ahead of time, refrigerate them, and reheat before serving. Serve with Sauce Bourguignonne.
